title: Oasiz Raises $2.5m to Revolutionise Gaming - Music Ally
text:
Two and a half million smackers for a tribute band? We can’t fault
their cover of ‘Cigarettes & Alcohol’
but that sounds a bit muc… Oh, wait, not THAT Oasiz. We’re talking about a US-based startup called
Oasiz
that launched this week with
the claim
that it “combines the viral momentum of TikTok with the creative depth of Roblox”.
What that means is a web-based service where “AI-powered tools enable users to generate games in seconds and share with the same speed and cultural resonance as memes or short videos”. So it’s about generating and remixing multiplayer games, with Oasiz pitching the idea that it’s now games’ turn to be given some user-generated content disruption.
Due to launch later this year, the startup’s $2.5m funding round was led by a16z Speedrun and The Venture Reality Fund. It’s part of a wider mini-trend around AI gaming: see our previous writeups of
Wolf Games
and
Little Umbrella
.
UGC is the key for Oasiz though: and if it takes off, perhaps there’ll be some opportunities for music and artists to be a (licensed) part of the platform.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
